mission.title: thanos: store: Store gateway consuming lots of memory / OOMing
mission.description: store: Store gateway consuming lots of memory / OOMing. This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify thanos troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your thanos deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n thanos --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review thanos configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get configmap -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=thanos
```
**Thanos, Prometheus and Golang version used**
thanos v0.1.0rc2

**What happened**
Thanos-store is consuming 50gb of memory during startup

**What you expected to happen**
Thanos-store does not consume so much memory for starting up

**Full logs to

steps.title: Apply the fix for store: Store gateway consuming lots of memory / OOMing
steps.description:
V2 of https://github.com/improbable-eng/thanos/pull/957/files

Doesn't involve any deletions & adds support for durations

https://github.com/improbable-eng/thanos/issues/814
```yaml
level=debug ts=2018-07-27T15:51:21.415788856Z caller=cluster.go:132 component=cluster msg="resolved peers to following addresses" peers=100.96.232.51:10900,100.99.70.149:10900,100.110.182.241:10900,100.126.12.148:10900
level=debug ts=2018-07-27T15:51:21.416254389Z caller=store.go:112 msg="initializing bucket store"
level=warn ts=2018-07-27T15:52:05.28837034Z caller=bucket.go:240 msg="loading block failed" id=01CKE41VDSJMSAJMN6N6K8SABE err="new bucket block: load index cache: download index file: copy object to file: write /var/thanos/store/01CKE41VDSJMSAJMN6N6K8SABE/index: cannot allocate memo
```

steps.title: Confirm store: Store gateway consuming lots of memory /… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n thanos --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n thanos --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
V2 of https://github.com/improbable-eng/thanos/pull/957/files

Doesn't involve any deletions & adds support for durations

https://github.com/improbable-eng/thanos/issues/814

See the source issue and linked PR for the complete solution details.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Thanos deployed (sidecar, query, store gateway, compactor as needed). The thanos binary provides subcommands for each component.
